_meta.title: LTX-2.3 IC-LoRA — Full Pipeline (I2V + V2V ControlNet + Motion LoRA)
_meta.description: Digital-Stud production workflow for LTX Video 2.3 with IC-LoRA identity conditioning. Covers three modes: (1) Image-to-Video with IC-LoRA identity, (2) Video-to-Video ControlNet motion transfer, (3) Motion LoRA camera control. Uses DiffSynth-Studio ComfyUI nodes. Generated run #54 (2026-03-13).
models_required: DiffSynth-Studio IC-LoRA weights (HuggingFace: ECNU-CILab/LTX-Video-2.3-IC-LoRA or custom trained)
Motion LoRA weights (optional, from DiffSynth community)
CLIP text encoder (standard, bundled with LTX-2.3)
_meta.digital_stud_use_case: Consistent-character video generation: same person/character across shots using IC-LoRA trained reference frames

nodes._comment: Load LTX-2.3 distilled FP8 for fastest iteration. For highest quality use ltxv_2.3_bf16.safetensors. FP8 tip: steps=8, CFG=1.0, disable LoRA during quick tests.
IC-LoRA weights. strength=1.0 for full identity lock; 0.7-0.8 for motion freedom. Bypass during V2V ControlNet if conflicts. Path: ComfyUI/models/loras/ic_lora/

widgets_values: A woman with dark hair walking through a sunlit urban street, cinematic motion, detailed face, natural lighting, 4K
nodes._comment: Positive prompt. Describe MOTION and ENVIRONMENT, not character appearance (IC-LoRA handles identity). Explicit motion words: 'walking', 'turning head', 'camera panning left'.

widgets_values: blurry, low quality, watermark, multiple people, deformed face, distorted anatomy
nodes._comment: Negative prompt. Always include 'multiple people' with IC-LoRA.
MODE 1: Load character reference image for IC-LoRA conditioning. Use SAME image across all shots for consistent identity.
identity_strength=0.85 (strong lock). reference_cfg_scale=0.0 (disabled; use 1.5-2.0 to strongly enforce reference). Increase to 0.95+ for closeup face-lock shots.
FP8 distilled: steps=8, CFG=1.0. BF16 full: steps=25-30, CFG=3.5-5.0. Fix seed for reproducible IC-LoRA identity. For 20s video: generate 5x ~4s chunks, concat with 1-2 frame overlap.
768x512 = 3:2 cinematic. 97 frames @ 25fps = ~3.9s. For 20s video: 5x this latent size. Dims must be divisible by 32. 24GB VRAM: up to 1280x720.

widgets_values:
=== MODE 2: V2V ControlNet (Motion Transfer) ===

Nodes to add:
1. VHS_LoadVideo → load source motion video
2. LTXVideoControlNetApply (ComfyUI-DiffSynth)
   - controlnet_model: ltxv_23_controlnet_depth.safetensors
   - strength: 0.6-0.8
   - start_percent: 0.0, end_percent: 0.7
3. Connect: LoadVideo → ControlNetApply → LTXVideoSampler
4. Keep IC-LoRA conditioning active in parallel

Result: source video = MOTION, IC-LoRA = IDENTITY
Source video person's appearance doesn't matter.
ControlNet 0.6 = loose; 0.85 = tight motion lock.

=== MODE 3: Motion LoRA Camera Control ===

1. Add LoraLoader BEFORE DiffSynthICLoRALoader
2. Load: motion_lora_pan_left.safetensors
   (available: pan_left/right, zoom_in/out, tilt_up/down, orbit)
3. strength_model: 0.5-0.8
4. Stack multiple motion LoRAs for combined moves

Sources: HuggingFace 'ltx-video motion lora'
         DiffSynth community
         Train custom with DiffSynth-Studio

Full combo: Motion LoRA (camera) + V2V ControlNet (body) + IC-LoRA (identity)
= who + what they do + how camera moves

=== TRAINING IC-LoRA ===

Dataset: 15-30 reference images
Varied angles/expressions/lighting
Resolution: 768x512

git clone https://github.com/modelscope/DiffSynth-Studio
pip install -e .

python train_ic_lora.py \
  --base_model ltxv_2.3_bf16.safetensors \
  --dataset_path ./my_character/ \
  --output_path ./ic_lora_output/ \
  --num_epochs 100 \
  --learning_rate 1e-4 \
  --rank 32 --batch_size 1 \
  --resolution 768 512

VRAM: ~12GB | Time: 2-4h RTX 4090
Output → ComfyUI/models/loras/ic_lora/

For video captioning of training data:
Use ComfyUI-DeZoomer-Nodes
(Video Captioning + Caption Refinement nodes)

digital_stud_notes.primary_use_case: Consistent-character video. Train IC-LoRA on 15-30 reference frames, use this workflow for all shots.
digital_stud_notes.sota_context: Best FREE LOCAL identity-consistent video. Commercial equivalent: Higgsfield Soul 2.0.
digital_stud_notes.pipeline_position: Step 4: FLUX.2 Klein char sheet → extract refs → IC-LoRA train → this workflow → SeedVR2 upscale → Z-Image Turbo face detailer
known_limitations: Identity drift on long sequences >8s — regenerate with fixed seed every 4s segment
V2V ControlNet + IC-LoRA: careful strength tuning needed (too high = identity leak)
FP8 distilled: CFG must be 1.0, steps 8 optimal
